title: GRIP
notes:
Doesn't launch - error message states "DX11 feature level 10.0 is required to run the engine."

Setting launch options to "PROTON_USE_WINED3D1=1 %command%" makes it launchable, but performance is very stuttery and there are quite a few graphical glitches (basically unplayable).

notes: Gameplay is fine with approximately 80% of native windows performance. But the music is very distorted, and audio volume controls do nothing.

title: GRIP: Combat Racing

notes: No issues launching and playing the game, but performance is extremely poor even on lowest settings. Some audio issues.
title: GRIP: Combat Racing

notes: Game runs normally, except performance is unplayably-bad.
title: GRIP: Combat Racing

notes: If you're experiencing poor frame rates on Nvidia, try spoofing an AMD card to improve performance (https://www.reddit.com/r/SteamPlay/comments/9m18ck/troubleshooting_performance/). Applying this workaround took my frame rate from single digits to a solid 55-60 fps. Tried to install xact to fix audio issues, but it didn't seem to have any effect.
title: GRIP: Combat Racing

notes: Performance much improved as of Proton 3.16-4 Beta. Installing winetricks xact to game prefix still has no effect (...compatdata/396900/pfx/), but for some reason replacing it with A Hat in Time's prefix folder + winetricks xact fixes the audio issues (...compatdata/253230/pfx/). Perhaps there is some prerequisite missing from Grip's redist files.
title: GRIP: Combat Racing

notes: Audio issues, multiplayer doesn't work. Performance is good.
title: GRIP: Combat Racing

notes: The game is messed up because its prerequisite installer does not work properly in Proton. If you have A Hat in Time installed you can simply swap its compatdata folder (253230) with GRIP's (396900) to get the necessary files. If not, you will have to download several installers from microsoft.com (directx_jun2010_redist.exe, dotNetFx40_Full_x86_x64.exe, vcredist_x64.exe, vcredist_x86.exe) and run them within the game's prefix via the wine control command. Either way you will also need to install winetricks xact to the prefix to get the sound working properly. Quite a hassle, but I can confirm both methods can get GRIP playable with perfect audio and full online multiplayer support in Linux.
title: GRIP: Combat Racing

notes: Works well with performance better than last time I tried it on windows. A couple of stutters the first time playing a track during when shader state cache is built, but subsequent plays are fine. Audio was also flawless with this Proton version. Nintendo Switch Pro controller also tested and working.The only issue I found was in audio/music options, when clicking on play to preview a music track it does nothing. This I believe however is a bug in the game on Windows too, so I'm still rating this platinum.
